quiz.title: Unit 2: Command of Evidence — Completing Claims with Evidence
quiz.subtitle: Selecting Data That Logically Completes a Given Claim (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question: Researchers studying sleep and memory found that participants who slept eight hours after learning a new skill showed 40% better retention than those who stayed awake. The data suggest that sleep enhances memory consolidation, as evidenced by ________.
options: no difference in retention between groups who napped versus those who slept fully
higher baseline memory scores among participants assigned to the sleep condition
a 40% retention advantage for the sleep group compared to the wake group
improved mood ratings in the sleep group that correlated with retention scores
questions.explanation: Choice C directly completes the claim by restating the key quantitative finding (40% advantage) that evidences consolidation. Choice A introduces an untested comparison (nap vs. full sleep). Choice B suggests pre-existing group differences, which would weaken the causal claim. Choice D cites mood, a confounding variable not central to the consolidation mechanism.

questions.question: A team analyzing urban air quality observed that neighborhoods with more tree canopy cover had lower average PM2.5 levels. The researchers concluded that vegetation filters particulate matter, a claim supported by ________.
options: higher property values in neighborhoods with extensive tree planting initiatives
increased pedestrian activity in green spaces during seasons with full foliage
a negative correlation between canopy percentage and PM2.5 concentration across sampled neighborhoods
similar PM2.5 levels in neighborhoods regardless of the species of trees planted
questions.explanation: Choice C logically completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (negative correlation) that supports the filtering hypothesis. Choice A addresses economic outcomes, not air quality. Choice B discusses human behavior, irrelevant to particulate filtration. Choice D suggests species don't matter, which doesn't directly evidence the vegetation-PM2.5 link.

questions.question: Economists tracking remote work trends noted that cities with higher percentages of remote workers saw smaller declines in downtown retail revenue during off-peak hours. This pattern suggests that remote workers support local businesses, as shown by ________.
options: a sharp increase in residential property values in neighborhoods with high remote-work adoption
declining commercial lease rates in central business districts following widespread remote-work policies
greater consumer spending on home-office equipment in regions with flexible work arrangements
a positive relationship between remote-work prevalence and off-peak retail revenue stability
questions.explanation: Choice D completes the claim by identifying the specific quantitative pattern (positive relationship) that evidences remote workers' support for local businesses. Choice A addresses housing markets, not retail revenue. Choice B describes a negative outcome for commercial real estate, inconsistent with the claim. Choice C focuses on equipment spending, not local business support.

questions.question: A study of exercise and cognitive function found that adults who engaged in moderate aerobic activity three times weekly showed improved executive function scores. The researchers attributed this to increased cerebral blood flow, a mechanism supported by ________.
options: higher self-reported energy levels among participants who exercised regularly
greater improvements in physical fitness metrics compared to cognitive test scores
similar cognitive gains in participants who practiced yoga versus aerobic exercise
a 22% increase in prefrontal cortex perfusion measured via fMRI in the exercise group
questions.explanation: Choice D logically completes the claim by providing specific physiological data (22% perfusion increase) that evidences the proposed blood-flow mechanism. Choice A relies on subjective self-reports rather than objective cerebral metrics. Choice B compares outcome domains without isolating the causal pathway. Choice C introduces an untested exercise comparison that doesn't address the blood-flow claim.

questions.question: Linguists examining dialect contact observed that communities with higher rates of intergroup marriage showed faster convergence in vowel pronunciation. The researchers concluded that social integration drives linguistic change, as demonstrated by ________.
options: a strong positive correlation between intermarriage rates and vowel-convergence speed
greater lexical borrowing in communities with frequent cross-dialect media exposure
stable consonant patterns despite changes in vowel systems across contact zones
younger speakers adopting merged vowels more readily than older speakers in all communities
questions.explanation: Choice A completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (positive correlation) that evidences social integration as the driver of linguistic change. Choice B introduces media exposure, a confounding variable not central to the intermarriage mechanism. Choice C addresses consonants, irrelevant to the vowel-convergence claim. Choice D describes age effects, which don't isolate social integration as the causal factor.

questions.question: Public health analysts found that regions implementing sugar-sweetened beverage taxes saw a 12% decline in pediatric obesity rates within three years. The analysts attributed this to reduced consumption of sugary drinks, a conclusion supported by ________.
options: a 19% reduction in sugary drink purchases among households with children in taxed regions
increased sales of untaxed beverages like bottled water and unsweetened teas in the same markets
stable obesity rates in neighboring regions that did not adopt beverage taxes during the study period
higher awareness of nutrition labels among parents in regions with public health campaigns
questions.explanation: Choice A logically completes the claim by providing the specific consumption data (19% purchase reduction) that evidences the proposed mechanism. Choice B describes substitution effects, which support the policy's impact but don't directly evidence reduced sugary-drink consumption. Choice C provides a comparison group, strengthening causal inference but not completing the mechanism claim. Choice D cites label awareness, a confounding variable.

questions.question: Ecologists monitoring forest recovery after wildfire observed that plots with higher soil nitrogen levels showed faster regrowth of native plant species. The team concluded that nutrient availability limits post-fire succession, a hypothesis confirmed by ________.
options: greater species richness in plots that received supplemental irrigation during the dry season
a 3.2-fold increase in native seedling density per unit increase in soil nitrogen concentration
similar regrowth rates across plots regardless of the severity of the initial fire damage
higher microbial activity in soils with elevated organic matter content following the fire
questions.explanation: Choice B completes the claim by specifying the quantitative relationship (3.2-fold increase) that evidences nutrient limitation as the mechanism. Choice A introduces water availability, a confounding variable not central to the nitrogen hypothesis. Choice C suggests fire severity doesn't matter, which doesn't isolate nutrient effects. Choice D addresses microbial activity, a related but distinct soil-health indicator.

questions.question: A cognitive science study found that participants who practiced retrieval (testing themselves) retained 50% more information after one week than those who restudied the same material. The researchers attributed this advantage to the 'testing effect,' as evidenced by ________.
options: higher confidence ratings among participants who used retrieval practice during learning
a 50% retention advantage for the retrieval-practice group compared to the restudy group
greater time-on-task for the restudy group, suggesting effort differences drove the outcome
similar performance on immediate post-tests but divergent scores on the one-week delayed test
questions.explanation: Choice B logically completes the claim by restating the key quantitative finding (50% advantage) that evidences the testing effect. Choice A cites confidence, a subjective measure not central to retention. Choice C introduces effort as an alternative explanation, which would weaken the testing-effect claim. Choice D describes a pattern consistent with the effect but doesn't complete the claim with the specific evidentiary data.

questions.question: Urban planners analyzing traffic patterns observed that intersections with dedicated left-turn signals had 35% fewer rear-end collisions than those with permissive left turns. The planners concluded that signal specialization reduces conflict points, a claim supported by ________.
options: increased overall traffic throughput at signalized intersections compared to roundabouts
higher pedestrian satisfaction scores in neighborhoods with upgraded traffic infrastructure
similar collision rates at intersections regardless of the time of day or traffic volume
a 35% reduction in rear-end collisions at intersections with dedicated left-turn signals
questions.explanation: Choice D completes the claim by specifying the quantitative outcome (35% reduction) that evidences the conflict-point reduction mechanism. Choice A addresses throughput, a different traffic metric not central to the collision-reduction claim. Choice B cites pedestrian satisfaction, irrelevant to vehicle collision patterns. Choice C suggests timing doesn't matter, which doesn't isolate signal design as the causal factor.

questions.question: Nutrition researchers found that participants who replaced refined grains with whole grains for eight weeks showed a 15% reduction in fasting insulin levels. The team attributed this to improved insulin sensitivity from increased fiber intake, a mechanism confirmed by ________.
options: greater weight loss among participants who reduced total caloric intake regardless of grain type
similar insulin changes in participants who increased vegetable intake without altering grain choices
a 15% decline in fasting insulin alongside a 28% increase in daily fiber consumption
higher self-reported satiety among those who consumed whole grains versus refined grains
questions.explanation: Choice C logically completes the claim by linking the insulin reduction to the proposed fiber-increase mechanism with specific quantitative data. Choice A introduces caloric reduction, a confounding variable not central to the fiber hypothesis. Choice B suggests vegetables drive the effect, which would weaken the grain-specific claim. Choice D cites satiety, a subjective outcome not directly evidencing insulin sensitivity.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately select data patterns that logically complete specific claims by evaluating mechanism alignment.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether the evidence directly addresses the exact mechanism or outcome stated in the claim.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: eliminate options that are true but irrelevant, or that support a different aspect of the argument.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: does this finding provide the specific quantitative evidence the claim requires to be complete?
